period_label: READY · IMPORT TO SEMRUSH POSITION TRACKING TO ACTIVATE
headline: Position Tracking · seed list ready
standfirst: The Task 9 keyword research is complete and the 35-keyword Position Tracking seed list has been published to the CMS Financial sheet. The chapter activates the moment Semrush Position Tracking ingests the seed list and the first weekly snapshot completes.
summary_lead: Task 9 done · seed list shipped. 35 curated keywords ready for Semrush Position Tracking — 5 brand, 10 commercial, 10 transactional, 5 informational, 5 Arabic. Drop the list into Semrush PT (UAE / desktop) and this chapter will start surfacing weekly position movements at the next monthly cycle.

win_tiles.label: PT seed list ready
win_tiles.tip: Curated 35-keyword list spanning all five intent categories — designed to give visibility into both brand-position protection and commercial growth movement.

win_tiles.label: Brand · Commercial · Transactional
win_tiles.tip: 5 brand keywords protect the existing top-ranking surface. 10 commercial keywords track the growth opportunity. 10 transactional keywords measure conversion-driver positions.

win_tiles.label: Arabic keywords tracked
win_tiles.tip: Five Arabic commercial and transactional keywords cover the AR-language slice of the GCC search universe — largely uncontested by competitors.

win_tiles.label: Refresh cadence
win_tiles.tip: Position Tracking refreshes positions daily, so weekly movement shows up here within 24-48 hours.

win_tiles.label: Full keyword universe
win_tiles.tip: The complete 95-keyword research output lives in the Keyword Master List tab. The 35 in PT are the priority subset; the broader 95 inform content planning and link-building anchor strategy.

win_tiles.label: Content briefs ready
win_tiles.tip: Six content briefs targeting the highest-value commercial gaps. Briefs #1 (How to Open) + #2 (CMA Regulation) map to existing April blog slots.

actions.title: Activate this chapter
actions.subtitle: One operator step + one ADNIKA cycle and weekly position movements appear here

rows.action: Open the 'Position Tracking Seed' tab in the CMS Financial sheet and copy column A (the 35 keywords)
Open Semrush → cmsfinancial.ae project → Position Tracking. Set parameters: Country = UAE, Device = Desktop, Language = English (auto-detects AR)
First snapshot completes within 24 hours of import
Next monthly cycle pulls the PT data into this chapter — position changes, visibility lift, top movers all surface here

prose.title: What the seed list is built to surface.
paragraphs: The seed list is composed for two simultaneous reads. The 5 brand keywords give us a leading indicator on brand-position drift — if 'cms finance' or 'cms financial uae' moves off #1, that's a signal worth acting on before it appears in monthly organic-rankings reports. The 25 non-brand keywords (commercial + transactional + Arabic) give us the growth signal: which engagement work moved which positions when.

The 10 commercial keywords target the realistic 90-day Tier-A and Tier-B ranges. These are the keywords from the master list with KD 30-45 that map cleanly to existing pages. As Authority Score lifts from the chapter 06 link-building campaign, these positions should start moving — and we'll see the movement here weekly before it shows up in any other report.

The Arabic keywords are the underserved opportunity. Five AR commercial and transactional keywords cover the highest-volume Arabic intent searches. Competitors largely don't optimize for these (none of cmsfinancial.ae's named competitors rank for Arabic queries). Once the AR content pipeline activates, this is where the wins will surface first.
